title: Canto 9 Chapter 12
summary: Complete English Bhagavatam chapter generated from the public gita/Datasets repository. Each route exposes one shloka at a time.
sourceNote: This chapter includes devanagari text, roman transliteration, English translation, and purport when available in the upstream corpus.

verses.text: श्रीशुक उवाचकुशस्य चातिथिस्तस्मान्निषधस्तत्सुतो नभ: ।पुण्डरीकोऽथ तत्पुत्र: क्षेमधन्वाभवत्तत: ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācakuśasya cātithis tasmānniṣadhas tat-suto nabhaḥpuṇḍarīko ’tha tat-putraḥkṣemadhanvābhavat tataḥ
verses.meaning: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: The son of Rāmacandra was Kuśa, the son of Kuśa was Atithi, the son of Atithi was Niṣadha, and the son of Niṣadha was Nabha. The son of Nabha was Puṇḍarīka, and from Puṇḍarīka came a son named Kṣemadhanvā.
verses.wordMeanings: śrī; śukaḥ; uvāca; kuśasya; ca; atithiḥ; tasmāt; niṣadhaḥ; tat; sutaḥ; nabhaḥ; puṇḍarīkaḥ; atha; tat; putraḥ; kṣemadhanvā; abhavat; tataḥ

verses.meaning: The son of Kṣemadhanvā was Devānīka, Devānīka’s son was Anīha, Anīha’s son was Pāriyātra, and Pāriyātra’s son was Balasthala. The son of Balasthala was Vajranābha, who was said to have been born from the effulgence of the sun-god.
verses.wordMeanings: devānīkaḥ; tataḥ; anīhaḥ; pāriyātraḥ; atha; tat; sutaḥ; tataḥ; balasthalaḥ; tasmāt; vajranābhaḥ; arka; sambhavaḥ

verses.text: सगणस्तत्सुतस्तस्माद् विधृतिश्चाभवत् सुत: ।ततो हिरण्यनाभोऽभूद् योगाचार्यस्तु जैमिने: ॥ ३ ॥शिष्य: कौशल्य आध्यात्मं याज्ञवल्‍क्योऽध्यगाद् यत: ।योगं महोदयम् ऋषिर्हृदयग्रन्थिभेदकम् ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: sagaṇas tat-sutas tasmādvidhṛtiś cābhavat sutaḥtato hiraṇyanābho ’bhūdyogācāryas tu jaimineḥ
verses.meaning: The son of Vajranābha was Sagaṇa, and his son was Vidhṛti. The son of Vidhṛti was Hiraṇyanābha, who became a disciple of Jaimini and became a great ācārya of mystic yoga. It is from Hiraṇyanābha that the great saint Yājñavalkya learned the highly elevated system of mystic yoga known as ādhyātma-yoga, which can loosen the knots of material attachment in the heart.
verses.wordMeanings: sagaṇaḥ; tat; sutaḥ; tasmāt; vidhṛtiḥ; ca; abhavat; sutaḥ; tataḥ; hiraṇyanābhaḥ; abhūt; yoga; ācāryaḥ; tu; jaimineḥ; śiṣyaḥ; kauśalyaḥ; ādhyātmam; yājñavalkyaḥ; adhyagāt; yataḥ; yogam; mahā; udayam; ṛṣiḥ; hṛdaya; granthi; bhedakam

verses.text: पुष्पो हिरण्यनाभस्य ध्रुवसन्धिस्ततोऽभवत् ।सुदर्शनोऽथाग्निवर्ण: शीघ्रस्तस्य मरु: सुत: ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: puṣpo hiraṇyanābhasyadhruvasandhis tato ’bhavatsudarśano ’thāgnivarṇaḥśīghras tasya maruḥ sutaḥ
verses.meaning: The son of Hiraṇyanābha was Puṣpa, and the son of Puṣpa was Dhruvasandhi. The son of Dhruvasandhi was Sudarśana, whose son was Agnivarṇa. The son of Agnivarṇa was named Śīghra, and his son was Maru.
verses.wordMeanings: puṣpaḥ; hiraṇyanābhasya; dhruvasandhiḥ; tataḥ; abhavat; sudarśanaḥ; atha; agnivarṇaḥ; śīghraḥ; tasya; maruḥ; sutaḥ

verses.text: सोऽसावास्ते योगसिद्ध: कलापग्राममास्थित: ।कलेरन्ते सूर्यवंशं नष्टं भावयिता पुन: ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: so ’sāv āste yoga-siddhaḥkalāpa-grāmam āsthitaḥkaler ante sūrya-vaṁśaṁnaṣṭaṁ bhāvayitā punaḥ
verses.meaning: Having achieved perfection in the power of mystic yoga, Maru still lives in a place known as Kalāpa-grāma. At the end of Kali-yuga, he will revive the lost Sūrya dynasty by begetting a son.
verses.wordMeanings: saḥ; asau; āste; yoga; siddhaḥ; kalāpa; grāmam; āsthitaḥ; kaleḥ; ante; sūrya; vaṁśam; naṣṭam; bhāvayitā; punaḥ
verses.purport: At least five thousand years ago, Śrīla Śukadeva Gosvāmī ascertained the existence of Maru in Kalāpa-grāma and said that Maru, having achieved a yoga-siddha body, would continue to exist until the end of Kali-yuga, which is calculated to continue for 432,000 years. Such is the perfection of mystic power. By controlling the breath, the perfect yogī can continue his life for as long as he likes. Sometimes we hear from the Vedic literature that some personalities from the Vedic age, such as Vyāsadeva and Aśvatthāmā, are still living. Here we understand that Maru is also still living. We are sometimes surprised that a mortal body can live for such a long time. The explanation of this longevity is given here by the word yoga-siddha. If one becomes perfect in the practice of yoga, he can live as long as he likes. The demonstration of some trifling yoga-siddha does not constitute perfection. Here is a factual example of perfection: a yoga-siddha can live as long as he likes.

verses.transliteration: tasmāt prasuśrutas tasyasandhis tasyāpy amarṣaṇaḥmahasvāṁs tat-sutas tasmādviśvabāhur ajāyata
verses.meaning: From Maru was born a son named Prasuśruta, from Prasuśruta came Sandhi, from Sandhi came Amarṣaṇa, and from Amarṣaṇa a son named Mahasvān. From Mahasvān, Viśvabāhu took his birth.
verses.wordMeanings: tasmāt; prasuśrutaḥ; tasya; sandhiḥ; tasya; api; amarṣaṇaḥ; mahasvān; tat; sutaḥ; tasmāt; viśvabāhuḥ; ajāyata

verses.text: तत: प्रसेनजित् तस्मात् तक्षको भविता पुन: ।ततो बृहद्ब‍लो यस्तु पित्रा ते समरे हत: ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tataḥ prasenajit tasmāttakṣako bhavitā punaḥtato bṛhadbalo yas tupitrā te samare hataḥ
verses.meaning: From Viśvabāhu came a son named Prasenajit, from Prasenajit came Takṣaka, and from Takṣaka came Bṛhadbala, who was killed in a fight by your father.
verses.wordMeanings: tataḥ; prasenajit; tasmāt; takṣakaḥ; bhavitā; punaḥ; tataḥ; bṛhadbalaḥ; yaḥ; tu; pitrā; te; samare; hataḥ

verses.text: एते हीक्ष्वाकुभूपाला अतीता: श‍ृण्वनागतान् ।बृहद्ब‍लस्य भविता पुत्रो नाम्ना बृहद्रण: ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: ete hīkṣvāku-bhūpālāatītāḥ śṛṇv anāgatānbṛhadbalasya bhavitāputro nāmnā bṛhadraṇaḥ
verses.meaning: All these kings in the dynasty of Ikṣvāku have passed away. Now please listen as I describe the kings who will be born in the future. From Bṛhadbala will come Bṛhadraṇa.
verses.wordMeanings: ete; hi; ikṣvāku; bhūpālāḥ; atītāḥ; śṛṇu; anāgatān; bṛhadbalasya; bhavitā; putraḥ; nāmnā; bṛhadraṇaḥ

verses.text: ऊरुक्रिय: सुतस्तस्य वत्सवृद्धो भविष्यति ।प्रतिव्योमस्ततो भानुर्दिवाको वाहिनीपति: ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: ūrukriyaḥ sutas tasyavatsavṛddho bhaviṣyatiprativyomas tato bhānurdivāko vāhinī-patiḥ
verses.meaning: The son of Bṛhadraṇa will be Ūrukriya, who will have a son named Vatsavṛddha. Vatsavṛddha will have a son named Prativyoma, and Prativyoma will have a son named Bhānu, from whom Divāka, a great commander of soldiers, will take birth.
verses.wordMeanings: ūrukriyaḥ; sutaḥ; tasya; vatsavṛddhaḥ; bhaviṣyati; prativyomaḥ; tataḥ; bhānuḥ; divākaḥ; vāhinī; patiḥ

verses.text: सहदेवस्ततो वीरो बृहदश्वोऽथ भानुमान् ।प्रतीकाश्वो भानुमत: सुप्रतीकोऽथ तत्सुत: ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: sahadevas tato vīrobṛhadaśvo ’tha bhānumānpratīkāśvo bhānumataḥsupratīko ’tha tat-sutaḥ
verses.meaning: Thereafter, from Divāka will come a son named Sahadeva, and from Sahadeva a great hero named Bṛhadaśva. From Bṛhadaśva will come Bhānumān, and from Bhānumān will come Pratīkāśva. The son of Pratīkāśva will be Supratīka.
verses.wordMeanings: sahadevaḥ; tataḥ; vīraḥ; bṛhadaśvaḥ; atha; bhānumān; pratīkāśvaḥ; bhānumataḥ; supratīkaḥ; atha; tat; sutaḥ

verses.transliteration: bhavitā marudevo ’thasunakṣatro ’tha puṣkaraḥtasyāntarikṣas tat-putraḥsutapās tad amitrajit
verses.meaning: Thereafter, from Supratīka will come Marudeva; from Marudeva, Sunakṣatra; from Sunakṣatra, Puṣkara; and from Puṣkara, Antarikṣa. The son of Antarikṣa will be Sutapā, and his son will be Amitrajit.
verses.wordMeanings: bhavitā; marudevaḥ; atha; sunakṣatraḥ; atha; puṣkaraḥ; tasya; antarikṣaḥ; tat; putraḥ; sutapāḥ; tat; amitrajit

verses.text: बृहद्राजस्तु तस्यापि बर्हिस्तस्मात् कृतञ्जय: ।रणञ्जयस्तस्य सुत: सञ्जयो भविता तत: ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: bṛhadrājas tu tasyāpibarhis tasmāt kṛtañjayaḥraṇañjayas tasya sutaḥsañjayo bhavitā tataḥ
verses.meaning: From Amitrajit will come a son named Bṛhadrāja, from Bṛhadrāja will come Barhi, and from Barhi will come Kṛtañjaya. The son of Kṛtañjaya will be known as Raṇañjaya, and from him will come a son named Sañjaya.
verses.wordMeanings: bṛhadrājaḥ; tu; tasya; api; barhiḥ; tasmāt; kṛtañjayaḥ; raṇañjayaḥ; tasya; sutaḥ; sañjayaḥ; bhavitā; tataḥ

verses.text: तस्माच्छाक्योऽथ शुद्धोदो लाङ्गलस्तत्सुत: स्मृत: ।तत: प्रसेनजित् तस्मात् क्षुद्रको भविता तत: ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasmāc chākyo ’tha śuddhodolāṅgalas tat-sutaḥ smṛtaḥtataḥ prasenajit tasmātkṣudrako bhavitā tataḥ
verses.meaning: From Sañjaya will come Śākya, from Śākya will come Śuddhoda, and from Śuddhoda will come Lāṅgala. From Lāṅgala will come Prasenajit, and from Prasenajit, Kṣudraka.
verses.wordMeanings: tasmāt; śākyaḥ; atha; śuddhodaḥ; lāṅgalaḥ; tat; sutaḥ; smṛtaḥ; tataḥ; prasenajit; tasmāt; kṣudrakaḥ; bhavitā; tataḥ

verses.text: रणको भविता तस्मात् सुरथस्तनयस्तत: ।सुमित्रो नाम निष्ठान्त एते बार्हद्ब‍लान्वया: ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: raṇako bhavitā tasmātsurathas tanayas tataḥsumitro nāma niṣṭhāntaete bārhadbalānvayāḥ
verses.meaning: From Kṣudraka will come Raṇaka, from Raṇaka will come Suratha, and from Suratha will come Sumitra, ending the dynasty. This is a description of the dynasty of Bṛhadbala.
verses.wordMeanings: raṇakaḥ; bhavitā; tasmāt; surathaḥ; tanayaḥ; tataḥ; sumitraḥ; nāma; niṣṭhā; antaḥ; ete; bārhadbala; anvayāḥ

verses.text: इक्ष्वाकूणामयं वंश: सुमित्रान्तो भविष्यति ।यतस्तं प्राप्य राजानं संस्थां प्राप्स्यति वै कलौ ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: ikṣvākūṇām ayaṁ vaṁśaḥsumitrānto bhaviṣyatiyatas taṁ prāpya rājānaṁsaṁsthāṁ prāpsyati vai kalau
verses.meaning: The last king in the dynasty of Ikṣvāku will be Sumitra; after Sumitra there will be no more sons in the dynasty of the sun-god, and thus the dynasty will end.
verses.wordMeanings: ikṣvākūṇām; ayam; vaṁśaḥ; sumitra; antaḥ; bhaviṣyati; yataḥ; tam; prāpya; rājānam; saṁsthām; prāpsyati; vai; kalau
